reviews.review_text:
This game is like slapping your balls, realising it hurts, but it just scratches a certain itch. You keep slapping them over and over again, then you start to squeeze. Then you squeeze again, and again, and again. Then you decide to just say ♥♥♥♥ it, crush them with a hammer. Well, no you have no testicles. Next best solutions? Move on to the penis and start the same process again. To say that I should've listened to the rest of the reviews is an understatement, playing this game is the equivalent of smashing your skull against a wall. Which is, unsurprisingly, a real outcome from playing this game. This makes the likes of Black Ice for Hoi4 seem trivial and less of a headache to play. 

I am on day 12 with at minimum 50 retries to my name, I hate everything to do with this game. This is like a toxic girlfriend, god she's a pain to deal with on a day-to-day basis and makes you consider committing a crime but the sex is just too good.

After 713 painful hours of Lobotomy Corporation I finally made it past Tiphereth Layer and now I’m going into Gebura with my brain basically fried beyond repair, but somehow I still highly recommend this game because it’s “super easy” and definitely won’t have you stressing over every little thing while your entire facility falls apart from one tiny mistake. Peak relaxing gameplay honestly.
this game will do what the title suggests. expect your brain to be nothing but mush by the end of it.

Firstly. I want to thank you very much for checking out Project Moon's origins and for trying to deepen your knowledge and your interest in the world and franchise the director has built by trying to experience the game yourself and not resorting to taking in risk at bias anecdotal heavy YouTube summary videos.

But I implore you to please, please!! Please!!! MOD this game. There is absolutely no shame in making this game NOT tedious for yourself! 

From an agent keeper mod, a macro mod, and a memory leak issue fix mod, to hell! those various amazing armor mods like the Rabbit Team Exosuit pack mod! Tweak this game to your liking! Make it less tedious and grindy! Because this game has NOT aged very, very well. And anyone who would try to shame you for not running this game purely vanilla is a moron who does not value time! YOU HAVE A LIFE TO LIVE! So do NOT waste your time grinding this game for the perfect "safe" facility. Do not waste days and restarts starting off from scratch because your best agents died before even the final lap to victory. This is why an agent keeper mod is in order! You can utilize them as they are without fear of losing them and having to grind through the tedious task of leveling them up again! Which is essentially the definition of insanity!

Modding is your responsibility. So please vet carefully and curate your fun! Just do not install blatant cheat mods like a default agent suit mod that has invulnerability and the standard poking stick dealing 999 damage!

I hope you have fun playing this game! Because fun is a priority! Meaningless hardship for validation is pointless! This game is old as hell. A fossil. An imperfect fossil with a good story to uncover but an unrealized, very flawed gameplay system! That does not account for player time!

You no longer have to boast about suffering because everyone knows PM games have casualized and the difficulty is inherently meaningless nowadays!

Again! Thank you so much! For being a responsible fan and playing the game yourself to form your own deeper connection to the franchise and the story the director is telling!

Do you like the SCP series? Do you like cute and dangerously unstable men and women? Well, this is for you. Welcome to Lobotomy Corporation. If you want a game where it throws everything at you all at once, then this is it. It rewards those who have the perseverance. Uh, oh, a small bird got out, looks like you have to suppress it. Then suddenly, all of those agents are dead. Looks like you have to start over again. It can be infuriating, yes. But the hard work does pay off.

It takes a special kind of brain damage to enjoy this game all the way through to the end. It does not respect your time in the slightest. It asks constant micromanagement and macro planning, and in the endgame you will wager half an hour of meticulous setup on a few seconds of tight execution. However, it is unbelievably satisfying to make progress, and having a game that not only rewards but requires the level of neurotic attention to detail that I'm going to do anyway is wonderful.

The UI is a little janky and the story is a little simple, and you can certainly tell that this is the studio's first game. But there's nothing else quite like it, and there's still something special about seeing the start of the Project Moon trilogy. This recommendation is highly conditional, but is given with a full heart regardless. Little Red and Punishing Bird are my ride-or-dies and they deserve the world.

Also don't believe the lies of the description and tags that call this a roguelike. It absolutely is not in any way.

This game has a very obvious filter, but if you can get past it, there's a rewarding experience on the other side.

To start with, the art style and music are very much standouts. Both make it easy to stay focused and play for hours on end, which is pretty much guaranteed due to the roguelike structure the gameplay follows. Not only that, but the abnormalities are quite interesting to interact with, and piecing each one together for the first time is a very novel experience.

Now, on the downside, the game can feel very clunky. Especially when combat turns mandatory, it's clear that the entire experience wasn't made with it as its forefront, so learning how to kite and take aggro from enemies feels more like playing around the mechanics than with them. The amount of information that's purposefully kept from you is also pretty high, leading to a big number of resets just because you blindly picked the wrong option and got instantly punished for it. And that's not even counting the times where you'll be resetting entire days just because of one bad RNG pull.

But this is a roguelike game, so most of these are actually features as opposed to failures on the game's part. If you can get into the rhythm of resetting days, weeks, or even the entire run and slowly learn how to adapt to each difficulty, then this is a very good time.

Idk this game spended all of my "lob"ed sanity so i have no idea what to type "fun" game trust definitely play this when you have a bad day cause later your going to realise that theres always that theres something that is going to make you have a even badder day (warming eats alot of your memories so if you have a god pc then not your problem if your a potato then remember to save and quit the game once in a while this is a bug theres a mod that fix this find it your self) have fun trying to manage this facility and your sanity.

This game was quite a rollercoaster as someone who came from playing Limbus Company. It's quite hard and I struggled often as the difficulty ramped up and more minute micromanagement was needed for specific Abnos and dealing with Ordeals in a timely manner. I was at least able to pick up on what was needed early and managed to go through missions quickly while often resetting before even getting past Briah. I spent my last 90 hours to 100% the game and repicking layouts to figure out a more easy setup for the final days.
I 100% recommend this game if you like difficult games and management sims. Though, the music may give you a lobotomy due to its lack of variation but that's part of the experience that makes you really a Manager.
This is also not gonna be 100% enjoyable because I had spent weeks on and off playing again and again after either getting ♥♥♥♥♥♥ over by my own failures or just bad luck. But the sense of fulfillment after struggling through difficult missions and parts of the game gave me the grit to keep going as someone who sucks at management sims.

I'm still fairly new to this game and just over 20 days in, and my cheeks have already been spread wide and filled by trains, worms, super meat boy's abusive drunk father, some necrotic feet, black ballsacks and a dog. I do not have high hopes for the rest of this game.

i love the universe, but this game is simply too hard for me, and i enjoy the gameplay but only to an extent, my sanity has been completely consumed by the mind ♥♥♥♥ that this game is, and i feel like i'm not even a third of the way done despite having put 50 hours on this game. this got me into project moon and i thank it for that, what a great story, but respectfully im just gonna go watch the rest of the story on youtube because i cant ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ deal with this ♥♥♥♥ anymore. im just gonna play it every once in a while...

Best game I can't beat after 187 hours of trying. Fun monster management sim where your AI secretary defiantly isn't tormenting your employees when you aren't looking and mass casualty events occur every Tuesday because I forgot about that accursed tree (you'll have no idea which one I mean, so just assume we agree on which one it is.) Many could probably beat the game much faster than me but I'm spiteful and far too attached to my agents to accept their deaths despite all my totally not crazy robot friends telling me I need to accept occasional blood sacrifices to end the day on a good note.
     Strongly recommend this game to the deranged managers who know that in the endless loop of resets it'll 100% be different this time and your favorite agent WILL succeed their work checks this time.

Have you ever enjoyed something so much that it sticks in your mind? This game has done that for me. Is there flaws? Of course there is, yet that's something I would gladly gloss over those. It's a nice build-up for the other games that Project Moon has to offer, and is basically large amount of lore dumping to understand this twisted Universe that they reside inside of.

I am in love.
For  a long time I've had an itch for SCP related games, but there was an issue, I knew all of them, all the proceedures, it took away all the discovery part, and those usually require decent hardware to run
WELL!
Lobotomy Corp has ALL of that covered! The monsters (Abnormalities) are incredibly interesting and unique, the lore it brings pulls you like a black hole and leaves you wanting to know it all. Do not misunderstand me though, you will hit you head on a brick wall quite often, until you finally figure out the gimicks of the thing giving you trouble, the artstyle subverts your expectations and has a lore reason for being this simple yet perfectly understandable blend of cute and eerie
If you like reading heavy games with a side of horror (a bit of every kind really) this is definitely worth giving a try, specially if you like the challenge late game runs bring
Peace, Frog

This game lives and dies by the RNG.
The first few tries are really fun, getting to know the monsters, the lore and the story. Sure it's slightly painful, but still fun.
But later game is all about challange, and nothing else really. Late game you could try the same day over and over again in the same way, praying for a better rng, and still get a worse one somehow. And its's so repetive, you will slip up the management out of sheer dullness. On top of that, there are countless first-time traps that will make you restart the day if you don't know about them beforehand.
That said, I can still recommend this game, if you are in a desparate need of a challange, as the lore and story is very good.
i love the gameplay, it may not be appealing for everyone but for people who likes a challenge it is worth buying
